characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Badass Adorable: They may have cute designs, but they're all extremely powerful. Cats Are Mean: Downplayed, as they are heroic but they still have their moments where they aren't nice to either their enemiesoreach other. In addition to that, the dub amplifies theirsnarkiness. Charles Atlas Superpower: They are a lot physically stronger than they appear to be. Color Motifs: They all wear colors that represent their personalities:Speedy: He has white armor which represents his growing potential as a fighter and a leader.Polly: She wears red armor which represents her fiery temper and passionate fighting spirit.Guido: He has blue armor which represents his laid back attitude and intelligence.Francine: She wears a green outfit which represents her down to earth nature and sensibility. Speedy: He has white armor which represents his growing potential as a fighter and a leader.

characters.name: The Group as a Whole
characters.description: Badass Adorable: They may have cute designs, but they're all extremely powerful. Cats Are Mean: Downplayed, as they are heroic but they still have their moments where they aren't nice to either their enemiesoreach other. In addition to that, the dub amplifies theirsnarkiness. Charles Atlas Superpower: They are a lot physically stronger than they appear to be. Color Motifs: They all wear colors that represent their personalities:Speedy: He has white armor which represents his growing potential as a fighter and a leader.Polly: She wears red armor which represents her fiery temper and passionate fighting spirit.Guido: He has blue armor which represents his laid back attitude and intelligence.Francine: She wears a green outfit which represents her down to earth nature and sensibility. Speedy: He has white armor which represents his growing potential as a fighter and a leader.

characters.name: Speedy Cerviche
characters.description: The team leader who sports white armor and mains two swords. He is an often lazy goofball who is still a competent hero when he needs to be. Act of True Love:In theGrand Finalehe directly pilots theSupreme Catatonic/Nyangokinginto an impending comet to save Little Tokyo without a second thought. Luckily he survives though.He's on the receiving end of this in the episode "Close Encounters Of The Pig Kind" whenthe alien pig he befriends eats so much food that he becomes gigantic and his enormous appetite threatens to cause a famine in Little Tokyo. Speedy can't bring himself to force the pig to return to space, so the pig willingly seals himself back into his spaceship and flies off the planet which reverses his transformation and saves Little Tokyo at the same time. He's on the receiving end of this in the episode "Close Encounters Of The Pig Kind" whenthe alien pig he befriends eats so much food that he becomes gigantic and his enormous appetite threatens to cause a famine in Little Tokyo. Speedy can't bring himself to force the pig to return to space, so the pig willingly seals himself back into his spaceship and flies off the planet which reverses his transformation and saves Little Tokyo at the same time. Calling Your Attacks: Particularly in the Japanese version:"Hissatu! NEKO ME SLASH!!" "Hissatu! NEKO ME SLASH!!"

characters.name: Polly Esther
characters.description: The token female of the group. Sports pink armor and mains a flute and heart-shaped ninja stars. She has an extremely short temper and hates laziness. A-Cup Angst: In the Japanese version, and mentioned in theSamurai Pizza Cats Official Fanbook, Polly was mentioned as having a smaller chest size and being insecure about it. In the manga she is downright overjoyed to tell Lucille her breasts are starting to grow bigger. This aspect goes unmentioned in SPC, as well as Discotek's DVD set. Ambiguously Bi: Is this in the English dub only. While she did fantasize about boyfriends and got into a relationship with Speedy in the final episode, she did compete in the tournament in episode 42 where the prize was a date with Princess Vi. Badass Adorable: Temper aside, Polly's quite the cute, lovely she-cat...who's also capable of ripping off a robot's armored plating with herbare hands. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: She's the brawn to Francine's brains and Lucille's beauty.

characters.name: Guido Anchovy
characters.description: Fellow Pizza Cat that sports blue armor and mains an umbrella. A haplessCasanova Wannabewho is nonetheless the most patient member of the group, often acting as a voice of reason when both Speedy and Polly go ballistic. Always Save the Girl: He's usually the first one to jump into action when a female is in danger. Amazon Chaser: Referenced during one instance where Polly and him are piloting the Supreme Catatonic and Polly threatens the Ninja Crows:Guido:(Thinking to himself) "Wow, when she takes charge like that, she's kind of....sexy!" Blue Is Calm: He wears blue armor and is the calmest and levelheaded member of the group. Blue Is Heroic: He's a hero that wears blue armor. He also uses blue fire to light up his sword when he does hisFinishing Move"Straight Fire".

characters.name: Francine
characters.description: The general manager of Little Tokyo's Pizza Cat restaurant andMission Controlthat sends the Pizza Cats to their destination. She is a loyal friend, though also extremely greedy and sometimes focuses too much on the pizza making side of their job. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: She's the brains to Polly's brawn and Lucille's beauty. Beware the Nice Ones: She may beMission Control, but if you mess up her date, she will lay the hammer DOWN. Just ask the giant robot from "Gender Bender Butterflies..." Big Beautiful Woman: How chubby she isvaries on the art style of the episode,but she is consistently portrayed as fairly heavy and is considered very pretty. Big Good:In the series finale, it's revealed that Francine/Otama's actually the head of the Cat Clan ninjas.

characters.name: Meowzma O'Tool
characters.description: Meowzma is a member of the Rescue Team with the ability to dig into the ground using his onboard drills and saw blades; he operates the Pizza Cat Restaurant in the Shinagawa District. Awesome Backpack: He carries a pair of enormous drills that he can use for burrowing. Blade Below the Shoulder: A pair of buzz-saws mounted in his gauntlets. Chainsaw Good: Well, more likeBuzz-SawGood, but the trope still applies. Dishing Out Dirt: Has some proficiency in using the earth itself as a weapon, as shown in his debut where he generates a cyclone of dust and rocks.

characters.name: Bat Cat
characters.description: Bat Cat runs the Pizza Cat Restaurant in the Fukagawa District. He has the ability to fly, which the Pizza Cats would later gain after they get the Goonie Birds. Awesome Backpack: Equipped with bat-themed glider-wings and a huge helicopter rotor. According to KNT's end-of-episode Ninja Scroll, they allow him to fly at speeds exceeding Mach 1. Blow You Away: By generating blasts of wind from his helicopter rotor. Book Dumb: Implied to be the case by Speedy in "Drumming Up Trouble With a Big Bad Beat", with his extensive knowledge of tongue twisters being brought about bybeing stuck in first grade for three years. Chick Magnet: He's very good with the ladies as shown in the episode "The Terror Of Prisoner Island".

characters.name: Spritz T. Cat
characters.description: Spritz operates the Pizza Cat Restaurant in the Tsukuda District. He has the ability to swim, and to use water-based attacks. Awesome Backpack: Capable of storing large amounts of water for his attacks. Cats Hate Water: Greatly subverted, as he's the go-to Rescue Team member for highly aquatic areas. A Day in the Limelight: He's the main character in the episode "Pizza Delivery Of Doom". Gatling Good: His backpack can combine with General Catton/Rikinoshin's to form a double-barreled water-spewing minigun.

characters.name: Sundance Kid
characters.description: The leader of the New York Pizza Cats. He is about as lazy and goofy as Speedy, whilst being even more arrogant. Americans Are Cowboys: His civilian attire and his use of revolvers all point to this. Blasting It Out of Their Hands: Given the demographic of the show, his use of firearms is limited to shooting the Ninja Crows' weapons out of their hands most of the time. Finishing Move: His "Cat's Eye Shooting", though it was never seen in the English versiondue to his use of guns. Gratuitous English: Michael's dialogue is this.

characters.name: Dee Dee
characters.description: The one female member of the New York Pizza Cats. A tall and flirtatious cat who uses a whip and her feminine wiles in battle. Heroic Seductress: Complete with a dominatrix getup (mask and high heels),a whip, andNoblewoman's Laugh.Madonna(after whipping all of the Ninja Crows): Call me, "Your Majesty"!Ninja Crows: Oh, Your Majesty! Ms. Fanservice: Both in and out of costume! She is probably the most curvy and gorgeous girl in the whole show. Named After Somebody Famous: She was named afterMadonnain the Japanese version. Pink Means Feminine: She has a pink top to compliment her feminine appearance.

characters.name: Cosmo
characters.description: A smooth and suave hero who is a legit casanova, unlike Guido. Blue Is Heroic: He's a hero who's armor has blue coloring. The Charmer: His first act upon showing up was to woo Polly for a minute. She never stood a chance. Lantern Jaw of Justice: He has a jawline as strong as his abilities. Named After Somebody Famous: He was named afterPrincein the Japanese version; his civilian uniform bears a resemblance to the one Prince wore inPurple Rain.

characters.name: Big Cheese
characters.description: The main antagonist of the series, he is a corrupt prime minister and Emperor Fred's second-in-command who plots to overthrow the royal family on an episodic basis. He is also a frequent crossdresser, and in KNT, adopted the alias, Ageyakko, when he dresses in drag. Ambiguously Bi: Despite hisCamp Gaycrossdressing antics being front and center, he's shown an attraction to the pop idol group Pointless Sisters/Mipple. Also, one of his plans involved using a Cupid robot to make Princess Vi/Usa fall in love with him. There is even more evidence in the Christmas episode’s English dub where he wanted Santa to give him a female dance partner for the New Years Eve Ball and dreamt that this partner was aDistaff Counterpartversion of him in a dress, which is different from the Japanese dub as in that dub, he wished for a dress. Berserk Button: He gets set off easily when people are disgusted with the results of his crossdressing. Beware the Silly Ones: Although theBig Badof the series, he's not treated very seriously.In the finale, he redirects a comet that would've blown Little Tokyo (and probably theentire planet) away had the comet not been stopped. Big Bad: His plots to overthrow Little Tokyo are the cause of most of the chaos the Pizza Cats have to face.

characters.name: Jerry Atric
characters.description: Big Cheese's second-in-command, he heads Bad Bird and the Ninja Crows. He is the one who comes up with most of their plans. Ambiguously Gay: His attitude towards Big Cheese’s flirting and antics is quite inconsistent as in some episodes, he is repulsed by it and in others appears receptive to it. Brains and Brawn: He's the brains to Bad Bird's brawn. Even Evil Has Standards: Jerry understandably became very disturbed after the Big Cheese revealed his master planto drop the Love Comet onto Little Tokyo in "The Big Comet Caper". The Evil Genius: He's one of the smartest members of the Big Cheese's army and gives him most of his plans and strategies to try to conquer Little Tokyo.

characters.name: Bad Bird
characters.description: Big Cheese and Jerry Atric's right-hand man who acts as the field leader to the Ninja Crows and is the enemy the Pizza Cats clash with the most. Accent Slip-Up: InKyatto Ninden Teyande, the voice Karamaru normally speaks in is not his natural voice. His actual voice has a thick Kansai accent (the equivalent of an American country accent) due to being raised in the country, which he briefly relapses into upon running into his childhood friend/lover Okara, who speaks with the same accent. The Ace: When he worked at Pizza Cat Restaurant in the episode, “Pizza Bird Delivers!” due to a need for money, he quickly proved much better at the Pizza Cats' jobs than them, to the point where Francine gave him a raise at the expense of the Pizza Cats' salary. Affectionate Nickname: Bad Bird's childhood girlfriend, Carla, refers to him as "Birdy". Alliterative Name: His name isBadBird.Averted at the end of the series when he makes aHeel–Face Turnand renames himself "Good Bird".

characters.name: Bad Max
characters.description: The leader of Rude Noise, an intimidating crow who is somewhat more sadistic than Bad Bird. Combat Pragmatist: With the Pizza Cats grounded and drained of their power, he deals with Bat Cat's arrival by getting his whole group to rush him in the air at once. Needless to say, Bat Cat didn't put up much of a fight. Dark Is Evil: He specifically refers to himself as "Zankaa of the Darkness." Dual Wielding: Wields a pair of tomahawks. Dude, Where's My Respect?: In the Japanese version, a recurring theme in later episodes is him (along with the rest of his team) trying to re-capture the fear and respect they had when they first showed up. Itreally sets him offwhenno one seems to remember him.

characters.name: Mojo Rojo
characters.description: BFS: His sword is a nodachi that rivals Bad Bird/Karamaru's! Evil Counterpart: To Bat Cat/Mietoru since they are the fastest members of their respective teams and are both specialists in aerial combat. Informed Ability: In the KNT version, he proclaims himself to be the fastest of the team. We don't get that many examples - if any - during the show itself. Lightning Bruiser: In KNT, he describes himself as the fastest member of Rude Noise. Meaningful Name: "Rojo" means "red" in Spanish.

characters.name: Cannonball Battery
characters.description: The Big Guy: His heavyset girth places him as this. However, it's more a case ofStout Strengththan being aFat Bastard. Evil Counterpart: To General Catton/Rikkinoshin since they are both the biggest members of their respective teams and the physically strongest members as well. Informed Ability: In the KNT version, he's described as being able to blow anything to bits with his cannon. In the show, he resorts to either melee combat or joining the rest of the team in their combination attacks. Meaningful Name:You think? Mighty Glacier: He's the biggest and physically strongest member of the group.

characters.name: Ronnie Geissmuller
characters.description: Evil Counterpart: To Spritz T. Cat/Nekkii since they are both specialists in underwater combat. Informed Ability: In the KNT version, he introduces himself by being an expert in underwater combat. Sadly, he never gets a chance to show this. Shout-Out: ToJohnnyWeissmuller.

characters.name: Emperor Fred
characters.description: Ditzy panda emperor of Little Tokyo and father of Princess Violet. Black Eyes of Crazy: Averted. His eyes have black sclerae and red irides, but he's a totally harmlessditz. It helps that his eyes arealways crossed. Catchphrase: "Fuh-RED!" (in SPC), and "Pafoo!" (in KNT), are all he can say normally. Comically Cross-Eyed: His perpetually crossed eyes reinforce his goofy personality. The Ditz: Is an utterly clueless twit who doesn't do any actual ruling.

characters.name: Princess Vi
characters.description: Spoiled rabbit princess and daughter to Emperor Fred. Extremely short tempted and often sends those who offend her to Prisoner Island. Abhorrent Admirer: Bad Bird would just as sooner fight the Pizza Cats than deal with Vi. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Or in this case all girls want bad birds. Subverted in the fact that she doesn't seem to care about Bad Bird's criminal activity, and only seems to like him for his physical appearance. Ambiguously Bi: Only in the English dub. In episode 42, she holds a tournament where the winner gets to go on a date with her and she allows both men and women to compete. The Japanese dub instead has the prize of a wish given to the winner instead And Now You Must Marry Me: A rare female example. She fully intends to do this to Bad Bird.

characters.name: Al Dente
characters.description: A just member of the royal court aware of Big Cheese's schemes. He is the boss of the Pizza Cats and gives them their missions. Animal Jingoism: Inverted. He's a dog, and he gets along just fine with the feline protagonists. Badly Battered Babysitter: Downplayed, as he's Princess Vi's royal caretaker and takes a lot of grief from her although he's one of the only characters that she showssomerespect towards. Big Friendly Dog: He's a big dog and a prettyNice Guyas well. Blue Is Calm: He's definitely the most levelheaded member of the royal court.

characters.name: Empress Frieda
characters.description: The Empress of Little Tokyo, Emperor Fred's wife, and Princess Vi's mother. She is known to have traveled across the world. Action Mom: She's a mother that can easily keep up with the heroes and the villains alike. Blow You Away: Played with, as hersneezesare powerful enough to blow all of the characters away like a hurricane. Charles Atlas Superpower: She's strong enough to tie a rope around Speedy, throw him across town to get her and Princess Vi some ice cream, and pull him back across town without any trouble at all. Cloudcuckoolander: She's just as quirky as she is energetic.

characters.name: Lucille
characters.description: The object of Speedy and Guido's affections and whom they usually fight for. She is a gentle girl who happens to have a set of missiles in her hair that fire whenever her emotions get the best of her. Very beautful, but not very bright. She owns and runs her teahouse and dessert restaurant, named "Mi-Chan" in the Japanese version. Ambiguously Gay: In the dub, the prize of Vi's tournament in episode 42 is to go on a date with her, and Lucille is one of the competitors. Baa-Bomb: A variation of this, as she's a sheep whose hair contains missiles. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: She's the beauty to Polly's brawn and Francine's brains. Berserk Button: Don't call her Missle Girl (in KNT).

characters.name: Guru Lou
characters.description: A perverted scientist who is responsible for giving the Pizza Cats access to the Supreme Catatonic, aHumongous Mechaused against larger enemy robots. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: He is normally a boastful old buffoon, but he is still an accomplished inventor and can give some good advice from time to time. He is also a surprisingly good fighter. Dirty Old Man: In the Japanese version, where he tends to be grabby towards females, especially Pururun. His lecherous traits were massively reduced in SPC, though he still is this from time to time.A notable example. In the Japanese version, he straight-up grabs Pururun's breasts in his first scene. Even by "Hentai no Baka" standards, his dialogue is pretty brazen, earning him the ultimate beatdown:Daisensei: (While feeling up a stunned Pururun) The two things I love most are girls and gambling! Hey... why don't we bet on the size of your first litter with me?In the Japanese version of "Youth is for Exploding", Daisensei leaps into Omitsu and begins grabbing her chest, causing her to fire her missiles at theMonster of the Week. A notable example. In the Japanese version, he straight-up grabs Pururun's breasts in his first scene. Even by "Hentai no Baka" standards, his dialogue is pretty brazen, earning him the ultimate beatdown: In the Japanese version of "Youth is for Exploding", Daisensei leaps into Omitsu and begins grabbing her chest, causing her to fire her missiles at theMonster of the Week.

characters.name: Momma Mutt and Junior
characters.description: Mother and son extras who make color comments while watching the Pizza Cats fly over them. Actor Allusion: In the Japanese version of "Princess Vi's Hippy Dippy Mom", Momma Mutt does an impression of Lucille;Yūko Mizutaniplayed both Momma Mutt and Lucille. Deadpan Snarker: Both of them engage in snide conversations about the Pizza Cats, usually after they are launched into action. Punny Name: Doubling asMeaningful Name, their names are clearly a play on the word "oyako" ("parent and child" in Japanese), right down to who's the parent and who's the child. Those Two Guys: In most episodes, their only real reason for being present is to comment on the Pizza Cats when they're getting launched. In theTime Travel Episode, Momma was the 'Junior' to her own mother.

characters.name: Carla
characters.description: Bad Bird's childhood girlfriend who performed as a clown at a traveling circus. Betty and Veronica: She's an old friend of Bad Bird's who he has a close and genuinely affectionate relationship with, versus Princess Vi being a bratty and controllingAbhorrent Admirer. Brooding Boy, Gentle Girl: She's the gentle girl to Bad Bird's brooding boy. Childhood Marriage Promise: She and Bad Bird promised that they would marry each other when they were young. The Cutie: She's aNice Girlwho's able to see Bad Bird for the goodness inside of him.

characters.name: The Ancient Warrior
characters.description: A mysterious warrior who Speedy faces while the latter trains for his rematch against Bad Bird. Dark Is Not Evil: He wears a black cape, and his armor is dusk-hued. However, helps out Speedy as aStealth Mentorandhelps him destroy the comet in the finale. Deus ex Machina:When things are looking hopeless in the finale, as Speedy and Bad Bird are face to face with a seemingly indestructible runaway comet, the Ancient Warrior suddenly appears on the Supreme Catatonic's monitor and reveals the secret of the mecha's ultimate power. In the Japanese version, he reveals himself as the creator of both the Supreme Catatonic and Speedy's Magical Ginzu Sword. Knight of Cerebus: A rare benevolent version. Things always get more serious when he appears. Non-Standard Character Design: In a world where everyone else appears in chibi-esque form, it's rather telling that the Ancient Warrior is drawn with more realistic proportions.

characters.name: The Narrator
characters.description: A witty off-screen narrator who tells the story of the series. Often takes part in the hilarity. Added Alliterative Appeal: He uses a lot of alliteration when describing the various perils of the Pizza Cats. Catchphrase: "Please allow me to explain" in the Japanese version. Deadpan Snarker: More snark than deadpan, since he has to keep the audience hyped. Infallible Narrator: Particularly in the Japanese version, often announcing a character's inner thoughts, or the truth behind a character's lie, whether unintentional or not. The characters usually don't notice. Either that, or they're just staying on script.

characters.name: Dr. Purple
characters.description: Appearing only in theFamicomvideo game, Dr. Purple is aMad Scientistowl who works for Ko'on-no-Kami (Big Cheese) in his latest plan. Amusement Park of Doom: He's first introduced in the video game using a trap-filled fun house to lure Animaloids inside, so they can be brainwashed into slave labor and made to construct a giant airship for Ko'on-no-Kami. Canon Foreigner: He only appeared in the Famicom game. Screw This, I'm Outta Here!: Decides to give up on villainy and go home after being defeated. In the English fan-translation, he leaves to go find work on thePower Rangersset. Shout-Out: The English fan-translation of the video game renames him"Dr. Whoot". His flying machine also resembles that used byDr. Eggman.
